microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's **recovery validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model used across multiple account teams.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation standard across cloud infrastructure.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating across engineering, IT, and security stakeholders.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly expanding the team's ability to support more customer accounts without added headcount.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows by conducting user demos and training sessions across 14 Azure service teams, translating technical capabilities into clear business value and saving 37 engineering hours monthly.

Influenced the product roadmap by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, resulting in intent-based YAML generation being prioritized as a core deliverable in the next planning cycle.

Improved **solution delivery speed** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform, cutting information lookup time by 83% across internal technical teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ engineering and operations teams.

Conducted discovery interviews with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort** per region launch, then built a strategic roadmap to automate critical paths and present solution recommendations to senior leadership.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to visualize operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps, prioritize high-impact investments, and communicate deployment health to executive stakeholders.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping software and hardware dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating rollout across IT and Engineering teams.

Drove strategic alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual intervention across distributed robotics infrastructure.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, stabilize the developer intake process, and maintain delivery velocity during a complex system transition.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a new CRM process that improved response time and customer-facing solution accuracy.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and Google Business profile to better reach local government and commercial clients.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and delivering data-driven recommendations that refined estimating models company-wide.

tailoring_notes: Wove in 'account team' and 'enterprise customers' framing (JD: 'join an account team') across microsoft_ft bullets 1 and 5 to mirror Esri's account-based sales motion.

Used 'cloud infrastructure,' 'network isolation,' 'IT and security stakeholders,' and 'hardware defects' in microsoft_ft bullets 3–4 to address the JD requirement for ArcGIS from an IT context (hardware, security, networking, cloud computing).

Embedded 'user demos,' 'translating technical capabilities into business value,' and 'solution recommendations to senior leadership' in microsoft_2023 bullet 1 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 to mirror JD language around demos, storytelling, and presenting to technical and non-technical customers.
Framed amazon_robotics bullet 1 around 'software and hardware dependencies' and 'distributed infrastructure' to echo JD requirements on geodatabases, DBMS, and system architecture troubleshooting.

Used 'go-to-market strategy' and 'local government and commercial clients' in pro_painters bullet 2 to surface local government domain awareness and sales/business development process experience called out in the JD.
